name: Cuisinart TOA-65 Digital AirFryer Toaster Oven with Convection

specs.Capacity: 0.6 cu ft interior — fits 12" pizza or 6 slices toast
specs.Functions: 8 — Air Fry, Convection Bake, Convection Broil, Bake, Broil, Warm, Toast, Speed Convection
specs.Accessories: Air fry rack, baking pan, broiling rack, oven rack

whatWorks: The TOA-65 genuinely replaces both a toaster oven and an air fryer without compromising either. The convection element produces crispy air-fried results that rival dedicated basket-style air fryers, while the full oven capacity handles a 12-inch pizza or a whole roast chicken without any awkward fitting.

Eight cooking functions cover every common use case — from the morning toast run to roasting a chicken to dehydrating herbs. Speed Convection is particularly useful: it cuts baking times by 30–40% compared to conventional oven settings.

The included accessories are genuinely useful: the air fry rack elevates food for 360-degree airflow, the baking pan catches drips, and the broiling rack handles steaks and fish. Most competitors charge extra for accessories that Cuisinart includes in the box.

Build quality is noticeably above the price point — the stainless steel exterior, solid door hinge, and consistent temperature calibration are all traits of a unit built to last. Owners who've had it 3–5 years consistently report it's still performing to spec.
worthKnowing: The exterior gets very hot during use — the top and sides reach temperatures that will burn you if you brush against them. If you have children in the kitchen, placement matters. The back needs at least 5 inches of clearance from the wall.

The digital controls require more button presses than a simple knob-and-dial setup — you select function, then set temperature, then set time. The learning curve is real for the first week, though it becomes intuitive quickly.
At 0.6 cubic feet, it's not a full oven replacement. A 9x13-inch baking dish doesn't fit — it tops out at 9x11. For holiday roasting or large sheet pans, you'll still need your main oven.

The crumb tray needs cleaning after every use if you use the toaster function heavily — crumbs build up quickly and can cause smoke if left to accumulate. Not a deal-breaker but a genuine maintenance requirement.

commonComplaints.title: Outside gets dangerously hot…
commonComplaints.body: The top and sides get extremely hot when in use. I've accidentally burned myself twice brushing by it. Not safe with small kids around without a permanent clear zone.

commonComplaints.title: Controls are fiddly to learn…
commonComplaints.body: Every function requires navigating multiple button presses. Took me two weeks before I stopped having to think about it. Would prefer physical dials.

commonComplaints.title: Can't fit a standard 9x13 pan…
commonComplaints.body: Listed as fitting a 12" pizza but a standard 9x13 baking dish doesn't fit. This is a meaningful limitation if you bake regularly.

commonComplaints.title: Crumb tray fills fast on toast mode…
commonComplaints.body: If you toast bread daily, the crumb tray fills up faster than you'd think. Left it too long once and it smoked. Clean it often.

verdict: The Cuisinart TOA-65 is the best counter-top oven for Canadians who want to consolidate an air fryer and a toaster oven into a single capable unit. The air fry function is genuinely good — not a compromise — and the full oven space handles most meals that a secondary oven would. The hot exterior and smaller-than-expected interior are real limitations, but for the use case of a compact secondary oven that also air fries, the TOA-65 is the benchmark.

realBuyers.quote: Replaced my separate air fryer and toaster oven. I've never looked back. Counter space reclaimed, and the food quality from this is actually better than my old basket air fryer.
Roasted a whole chicken in it last night. Skin was perfectly crispy, meat was juicy. I use my main oven maybe once a month now.
The air fry rack is the key — food actually elevates off the surface and crisps on all sides. That's what's missing from cheap air fryers.
Had mine for three years. Still calibrates perfectly, door hinge is tight, no issues. Built to last at a reasonable price.

sources.title: Cuisinart TOA-65 AirFryer Toaster Oven — Amazon.ca

metaDescription: Cuisinart TOA-65 AirFryer Toaster Oven review for Canada: 8 functions, fits 12" pizza, replaces air fryer + toaster oven. Honest on hot exterior. ~$249 CAD.
introText: The Cuisinart TOA-65 is a full-featured digital air fryer toaster oven that combines eight cooking functions — including genuine convection air frying — in a 0.6 cubic foot stainless steel unit. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B07P24W6GV) at around $249 CAD.
